title: Aardvark/Netavark flakes
body:
```
Aardvark Test 2: Two containers, same subnet
...

Expected
               <[]string | len:0, cap:0>: nil
           to have length 2
         
           /var/tmp/go/src/github.com[/containers/podman/test/e2e/run_aardvark_test.go:105](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/bb8f53a727aeebb49873b2f4c9c12d6f1d0ce1d3/test/e2e/run_aardvark_test.go#L105)
```

comments.body: This is flaking [a lot lately](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/4750591279759360/html/int-podman-fedora-36-root-host.log.html). Can a network person PTAL.

I am unable to reproduce this flake on host not entirely sure but I think i know why this happens on CI one reason it below but there can be more reasons for it.

One root cause could be that  `netavark` spawns `aardvark-dns` using `Command::spawn()` and never waits for it especially in case of `systemd-run`. Usually it does not makes any difference since by the time podman actually runs command on container , `aardvark-dns` should be ready but on CI env on certain runs we are surpassing this window.

I think adding a backoff on netavark end should help here. Something like this: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/14173

Above case is specially for the test `[It] podman run check dnsname plugin with Netavark` where this can happen more often.

PS: Also while running tests on host I am only running `test/e2e/run_networking_test.go test/e2e/run_aardvark_test.go` with commons assuming that tests are isolated with each other they share nothing.

There is a bigger design change under discussion which should help in this as well as some other issue, please track it here. However it will take some time to get merged: https://github.com/containers/aardvark-dns/pull/148

The netavark/aardvark changes are fine but I don't think it is required to fix this flake in CI. The test already "tries" to implement a back off logic:
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/6dffa453a4c62a183526e22ac5c099f02b6c6f0e/test/e2e/common_test.go#L1045-L1062

This does not work because dig always returns exit code 0 even when there is no match.

Netavark/aardvark change is for more scenarios like https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/14356#issuecomment-1154649763 i am not able to collect all the reference but it was reported few times that on the first container run dns resolve failed intermittently. But we can close these issues early if dig output match is fixed.

I'm still seeing flakes on this test, and I'm somewhat sure that it's after the "fix" merged. Error message seems different:
```
dns is not responding
```
Shall I file a new issue?

### Podman run networking [It] Aardvark Test 2: Two containers, same subnet 
* fedora-36 : int podman fedora-36 root host
  * PR #14841
    * [07-06 11:47](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/6095397528535040/html/int-podman-fedora-36-root-host.log.html#t--Aardvark-Test-2--Two-containers--same-subnet--1)
    * [07-06 11:47](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/6095397528535040/html/int-podman-fedora-36-root-host.log.html#t--Aardvark-Test-2--Two-containers--same-subnet--2)
    * [07-06 11:47](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/6095397528535040/html/int-podman-fedora-36-root-host.log.html#t--Aardvark-Test-2--Two-containers--same-subnet--3)
Yeah something is wrong, I have to add more debugging information but at this point it does not look like a race. There should be more than enough timout.

New set of symptoms in [remote f36 root](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6300287030788096/html/int-remote-fedora-36-root-host.log.html):
```
Execing /usr/sbin/ip netns add XVlBzgbaiCMR

Cannot create namespace file "/var/run/netns/XVlBzgbaiCMR": File exists
```
The surprising thing about this is that a *lot* of tests fail, and all of them have the same presumably-random string. I would expect each test to have a unique string. Whoever groks the random-netns-name code, that might be a good starting place to look.
Renaming & repurposing this issue as a general place to log \*vark flakes

> New set of symptoms in [remote f36 root](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6300287030788096/html/int-remote-fedora-36-root-host.log.html):
> 
> ```
> Execing /usr/sbin/ip netns add XVlBzgbaiCMR
> 
> Cannot create namespace file "/var/run/netns/XVlBzgbaiCMR": File exists
> ```
> 
> The surprising thing about this is that a _lot_ of tests fail, and all of them have the same presumably-random string. I would expect each test to have a unique string. Whoever groks the random-netns-name code, that might be a good starting place to look.

This should not effect netavark/aardvark at all, using a custom net namespace will never call into the network backend.
Another one, this time [podman (non-remote) f36 root](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5684895023366144/html/int-podman-fedora-36-root-host.log.html), same "file exists" symptom
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Last seen November 10. I will assume this is fixed.
